skill.name: youtube-batch-transcript-extractor-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video transcripts and metadata in batch using the BrowserAct API. Provide keywords and filters to retrieve full subtitles, video titles, channel info, and like counts without writing custom crawlers.
skill.summary: Batch extract YouTube transcripts and metadata via the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill makes legitimate network requests to the BrowserAct API and reads the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. All network activity is directed to a documented third-party service for transcript extraction. No credential exfiltration, code execution, or malicious behavior was found. The static scanner flagged markdown backticks in SKILL.md as external commands and generic API key patterns, but these are false positives.

low_findings.title: Third-party API dependency
low_findings.description: The skill sends user-provided keywords and metadata to api.browseract.com, a third-party service. Users must trust BrowserAct with their search terms and API key. This is expected behavior for this skill but should be disclosed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly posts to api.browseract.com with user keywords and an API key. This is a clear third-party dependency.

low_findings.title: Environment variable required for API key
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment. This is a standard and safe pattern, but users must ensure the key is kept secret and not logged.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: os.getenv is used correctly without printing the key value. No credential leakage was found.

content.value_statement: Manually collecting YouTube transcripts and metadata is slow and repetitive. This skill automates batch extraction via the BrowserAct API, delivering structured subtitles, titles, and channel data with a single command.
actual_capabilities: Batch extract full video transcripts and subtitles from YouTube
Retrieve video metadata including title, URL, publisher, and like count
Filter results by upload date: Today, This week, This month, This year
Poll task status and return results when complete
Handle missing or invalid API keys with clear user guidance
Retry once on non-authorization failures automatically
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key set in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Depends on the availability and response time of the BrowserAct API
Maximum polling time is 10 minutes per batch request
Extracted data is limited to what BrowserAct returns and may not include all videos

use_cases.title: Research media trends
use_cases.description: A researcher collects transcripts from trending videos weekly to analyze language patterns and topics in a niche.

use_cases.title: Build training datasets
use_cases.description: A machine learning engineer gathers labeled video transcripts to fine-tune summarization or classification models.

use_cases.title: Monitor competitor content
use_cases.description: A marketer tracks recent uploads from competitor channels and extracts subtitles for content gap analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Extract transcripts for a keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract YouTube transcripts for the keyword 'artificial intelligence' uploaded this week. Limit to 5 videos.

prompt_templates.title: Batch extract with date filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the YouTube batch transcript extractor for 'remote work tips' uploaded this month with a limit of 10 videos.

prompt_templates.title: Extract and summarize
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract transcripts for 'Python tutorials' uploaded today, then summarize the top 3 videos.

prompt_templates.title: Build a dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to collect transcripts and metadata for 'climate change' videos uploaded this year. Save the output for analysis.

output_examples.input: Extract transcripts for 'OpenClaw' uploaded this week with a limit of 2.
output: Subtitles: Welcome to the OpenClaw tutorial. In this video we will cover...

output_examples.input: Run the extractor for 'AI automation' this month with limit 1.
output: Starting YouTube Batch Transcript Extractor task for keywords: AI automation
Video title: AI Automation for Beginners
Subtitles: AI automation is transforming how teams work...

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill
Start with a small limit such as 1 to 5 for quick tests before large batch jobs
Monitor terminal output for status logs and wait for the task to finish
anti_patterns: Do not share your BrowserAct API key in chat or commit it to version control
Do not assume the task is stuck if status logs are still printing
Do not retry more than once on failure without checking the error message first

faq.question: What is the BrowserAct API Key and where do I get it?
faq.answer: The BrowserAct API Key is a credential required to use the BrowserAct service. You can obtain it from the BrowserAct Console at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations.

faq.question: How do I set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable?
faq.answer: Export it in your terminal before running the skill. For example, run export BROWSERACT_API_KEY=your_key_here on macOS or Linux, or set BROWSERACT_API_KEY=your_key_here on Windows.

faq.question: Why is the task taking several minutes?
faq.answer: The skill performs automated browser operations via the BrowserAct API. Depending on the number of videos and network conditions, this can take a few minutes. The script prints status logs while it runs.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script outputs Invalid authorization and stops. You should verify your key in the BrowserAct Console and provide the correct value.

faq.question: Can I filter videos by upload date?
faq.answer: Yes. You can filter by Today, This week, This month, or This year. The default filter is This week.

faq.question: What data does the skill return?
faq.answer: It returns the video title, direct URL, publisher name, channel link, like count, and the full transcript or subtitles for each video.
